methods.description: Calculation of life table parameters using the age-stage, two-sex life table procedure to analyze survival and fecundity rates across different generations and diets.

steps.description: The values of all life table parameters, including the net reproductive rate (R 0 ), gross reproductive rate (GRR), finite rate of increase (λ), intrinsic rate of increase (r), and mean generation time (T), as well as age-stage-specific survival rate (s xj ), age-specific survival rate (l x ) (the probability that a newborn will survive to age x, calculated by pooling all of the surviving individuals of different stages), age-stagespecific fecundity (f xj ), and age-specific fecundity (m x ) were calculated according to the age-stage, two-sex life table procedure (Chi andLiu 1985, Chi 1988) using the TWOSEX-MSChart software (Chi 2021) at different generations on different diets.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate life table parameters using TWOSEX-MSChart software

steps.description: The variances and standard errors of all life table parameters were estimated using the bootstrap procedure with 100,000 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate variances and standard errors using bootstrap with 100,000 samples

steps.description: Multiple comparisons among different generations of each diet and the corresponding generation among different diets were carried out using the paired bootstrap test (Reddy andChi 2015, Bahari et al. 2018).
steps.searchable_summary: perform paired bootstrap test for multiple comparisons
